toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-723c48e2656c479c886d8efcfac0fa76 && \
  mkdir -p CrashDumps && \
  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="$(pwd)/CrashDumps/coredump.%p.%t" \
  dotnet test --no-pager 2>&1
arguments.description: Run dotnet tests with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet tests with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
This is a .NET 9 MSTest project. The user wants to run the tests and collect crash dumps if the test host crashes. 

For .NET, the standard way to collect crash dumps on crash is to set environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables mini dump collection
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — full dump (MiniDumpWithFullMemory). Options: 1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` — where to write the dump


- Now I'll execute the tests with these environment variables configured to capture any crash dumps.

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-723c48e2656c479c886d8efcfac0fa76 && \
  mkdir -p CrashDumps && \
  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="$(pwd)/CrashDumps/coredump.%p.%t" \
  dotnet test --no-pager 2>&1

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's what I ran:

```
DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="CrashDumps/coredump.%p.%t" \
dotnet test
```

**Environment variables used for crash dump collection:**

| Variable | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` | `1` | Enables automatic dump on crash |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType` | `4` | Full memory dump |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` | `CrashDumps/coredump.%p.%t` | Dump path with PID + timestamp |

No crashes occurred, so the `CrashDumps/` directory is empty. If the test host had crashed, a core dump would have been written there automatically by the .NET runtime.
